question: What is the difference between a call option and a put option?
answer: A call option gives its buyer the right to BUY the underlying asset at the strike price, and is used when the buyer believes the price will go UP [doc_0:page_8]. In contrast, a put option gives its buyer the right to SELL the underlying asset at the strike price, and is used when the buyer believes the price will FALL [doc_1:page_10].
